definition.pre_launch: A governed service remains pre-launch while operational readiness is incomplete, even if the implementation appears feature complete.
definition.launch_ready: A governed service may be called launch-ready only when rollback, operator ownership, current evidence, governance boundary seal, current auth and approval receipts, and reflection-cleared execution readiness are all in place for production exposure.

notes: The service must have an operational runbook reference that is current enough to trust.
A rollback path must already be written and reviewable before launch-ready is granted.
A named operator must actively own the service at launch time.
The evidence packet must be recent enough that the launch verdict is not stale.
Production work must have a valid pre-release scan before launch-ready can be granted.
The linked service must declare a governance kernel path and profile.
A current baseline packet must exist with dependency, build, test, Lighthouse budget, and security status evidence.
governance/plan.json must be approved before apply execution is allowed.
Apply intent must be constrained to governance/** unless a scoped external cleanup receipt explicitly allows more.
An active auth contract must exist and remain unexpired for the release path.
An unexpired approval receipt must exist whenever cleanup reaches outside governance/.
Actual cleanup scope must not exceed the approved mutation scope.
No open high-severity reflection action may remain overdue or unresolved.
The full agent to governance verdict protocol chain must be present and trace-linked.
The shell contract must remain valid and constrained by architecture, approval, and rollback context.
Human and agent ideation must be present as trace-linked collaboration packets.
A minimum service value unit and buyer problem statement must exist before launch-ready is granted.
No unresolved blocking risk may remain when the launch-ready verdict is issued.
The service must have a passing DTP validator receipt that matches the current trace head before launch-ready is granted.
The service must have a hygiene clearance receipt and may not be in cleanup-required status.

anti_patterns: Calling a service launch-ready because the UI looks finished while operational ownership is still unclear.
Calling a service launch-ready without a rollback plan.
Using launch-ready as a substitute for market proof.
Calling a service launch-ready before governance/plan.json approval is recorded.
Calling a service launch-ready when the auth contract is expired or revoked.
Calling a service launch-ready when the approval receipt is expired, missing, or broader than the executed cleanup scope.
Calling a service launch-ready when the protocol chain is incomplete or shell execution lacks architecture context.
Calling a service launch-ready when no minimum commercialization path is defined.
Calling a service launch-ready while high-severity reflection debt is still open or overdue.
Calling a service launch-ready while service hygiene is in cleanup-required status.
